text: Assume this can be a hundred thousand tasks. The repeated scans won't scale. How would you improve it?
I'd build indegree counts and reverse dependency edges, then keep currently ready tasks in a heap ordered by priority and original position.
Good. Please modify the existing solution. Don't lose the malformed-input behavior or blocked reasons.
Right. I need to preserve `normalize_tasks`, the output contract, stable ties, and the simplified missing-versus-cycle rule.
Exactly. Also, the code is spread across several screens now; use the current screenshots together, not the earlier version.
Understood. I'll replace only the scheduling core with the graph and heap structures.
